samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 2*size bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size" value="4">Number of bits in the multiplicand and multiplier.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="mul_a_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extends the input signal mul_a by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.</assign>
        <assign id="mul_b_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extends the input signal mul_b by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.</assign>
        <genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < size; i=i+1) begin : gen_partial_products
                <assign id="partial_product[i]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the partial product based on the corresponding bit in the multiplier (mul_b).</assign>
            end
        endgenerate
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="register[0][0]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the first level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        <reg id="register[0][1]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the second level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        <reg id="register[0][2]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the third level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        <reg id="register[0][3]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the fourth level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        <reg id="register[1][0]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the fifth level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        <reg id="register[1][1]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the sixth level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        <reg id="register[1][2]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the seventh level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        <reg id="register[1][3]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the eighth level register for storing intermediate values during the multiplication process.</assign>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                // Reset all registers to 0
                register[0][0] <= 0;
                register[0][1] <= 0;
                register[0][2] <= 0;
                register[0][3] <= 0;
                register[1][0] <= 0;
                register[1][1] <= 0;
                register[1][2] <= 0;
                register[1][3] <= 0;
            end else begin
                // Add operations for each stage
                register[0][0] <= partial_product[0];
                register[0][1] <= register[0][0] + partial_product[1];
                register[0][2] <= register[0][1] + partial_product[2];
                register[0][3] <= register[0][2] + partial_product[3];
                
                register[1][0] <= register[0][0];
                register[1][1] <= register[0][1];
                register[1][2] <= register[0][2];
                register[1][3] <= register[0][3];
            end
        end
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                // Reset product output to 0
                mul_out <= 0;
            end else begin
                // Calculate final product
                mul_out <= register[1][0] + register[1][1] + register[1][2] + register[1][3];
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <description></description>
    </task>
</module>
